trace_target.label: egress policy — dnsmasq + nftables (fail-closed, IPv4/IPv6 parity, atomic reload)

requirements.title: dnsmasq is the in-container DNS authority; /etc/resolv.conf points only at 127.0.0.1:53; upstream fail-open resolv.conf gap is closed
substrate_evidence: packages/devbox/dnsmasq/dnsmasq.conf authored iter-158 (2592 bytes): listen-address=127.0.0.1,::1 + port=53 + bind-interfaces + user=nobody/group=nogroup (privilege-drop post-bind) + no-hosts + no-resolv + stop-dns-rebind + domain-needed + bogus-priv hardening block + address=/#/0.0.0.0 + address=/#/:: fail-closed defaults + log-queries=extra + log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log (SC-3 JSONL emitter feed) + KEEL_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST_MARKER_{START,END} placeholder block for reload-time server=/<domain>/$UPSTREAM injection per SC-12.

packages/devbox/scripts/start-egress.sh authored iter-158 (4767 bytes; 0755; #!/usr/bin/env bash; set -euo pipefail; bash -n exit 0 at iter-159 verification): overwrites /etc/resolv.conf to exactly two lines 'nameserver 127.0.0.1' + 'options edns0 single-request-reopen' per SC-13 (closes upstream cc-devbox fail-open 8.8.8.8 fallback); mkdir -p /workspace/logs + /run (SC-17 idempotent pre-create); composes initial whitelist at /run/keel-whitelist.composed.txt (whitelist.default.txt + whitelist/*.txt concatenation with comment/blank strip); invokes scripts/reload-egress.sh /run/keel-whitelist.composed.txt; launches egress-log-tailer.sh via nohup + PID capture to /run/keel-egress-tailer.pid; verifies dnsmasq liveness via 5s retry loop (pgrep -x dnsmasq || ss -lnp | grep ':53') → fail-hard (exit 1) if not up per AC 5 fail-closed-no-silent-allow.

packages/devbox/.envrc.example iter-158 line 43-44: '# --- Egress policy (Story 2.3) ---' section + 'KEEL_DEVBOX_DNS_UPSTREAM=1.1.1.1  # Upstream resolver for whitelisted domains. Cloudflare default; operator may retune to corporate resolver. Consumed by packages/devbox/scripts/reload-egress.sh.' per SC-14 (120-char budget; single new knob — no whitelist-source knob per Story 2.4 boundary; no rotation-size knob per SC-4 pin).

packages/devbox/Dockerfile lines 56-57: 'dnsmasq' + 'nftables' appended to the existing apt-get install layer per SC-1 + Story 2.1 single-layer discipline (same RUN block as Story 2.1's system-packages; apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* discipline preserved per Story 2.1 dev-story pattern). Binaries available in baked image post-next-bake.

packages/devbox/entrypoint.sh line 101: 'Story 2.3: fail-closed egress policy (dnsmasq + nftables). Hard-fail if init fails.' comment + single new block per SC-11 invoking /workspace/packages/devbox/scripts/start-egress.sh via absolute bind-mount path; explicit fail-hard exit 1 with actionable stderr if script is missing or non-executable (fail-closed posture per NFR6). Inserted AFTER Story 2.1 workspace-owner chown + OAuth volume dir bring-up; BEFORE exec "$@" / sleep infinity tail per SC-11 surgery-discipline.

Live-smoke AC 1 runtime verification (Task 12.1 substrate — docker exec devbox cat /etc/resolv.conf | grep -q '^nameserver 127\.0\.0\.1$' + pgrep -x dnsmasq) deferred to operator workstation per backend-B iteration-env constraint (Story 2.1 iter-127 bind-mount-denial precedent); SC-13 verbatim command pinned in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § Verification + packages/devbox/README.md § Egress policy § Verification for operator close-out pass.

requirements.title: nftables default policy is DROP for both IPv4 (ip filter output) and IPv6 (ip6 filter output) filter output chains; upstream IPv6 gap is closed (policy parity verified by in-container test)
substrate_evidence: packages/devbox/nftables/egress.nft authored iter-158 (3605 bytes): single 'table inet keel_egress' with two chains — 'chain output_v4 { type filter hook output priority 0; policy drop; ... }' + 'chain output_v6 { type filter hook output priority 0; policy drop; ... }'. Both chains hooked at identical priority per SC-7. SC-7 verbatim commands (`nft list chain inet keel_egress output_v4 | grep -q 'policy drop'` + `nft list chain inet keel_egress output_v6 | grep -q 'policy drop'`) pinned in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § Verification.

Non-obvious nftables chain-scope pattern applied iter-158: 'meta nfproto != <family> accept' short-circuit as FIRST rule of each chain (not a policy-level filter). In inet-family tables with two chains hooked at identical priority, both chains evaluate EVERY packet from BOTH families — the scope-via-'meta nfproto' pattern the v1.1 draft implied would double-drop non-target-family packets via each chain's policy drop. Accept-fast first rule preserves per-chain family scope while SC-7 grep -q 'policy drop' assertion still passes because chain policy is unchanged. Decision pinned in story file v1.3 Change Log + Completion Notes § Known residual risk.

Baseline allow rules in both chains: ct state established,related accept (preserves established-connection preservation across atomic reload per SC-5 commentary); udp dport 53 ip daddr 127.0.0.1 accept + udp sport 53 ip saddr 127.0.0.1 accept (dnsmasq loopback DNS reply path per Task 4.1); KEEL_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST_MARKER_{START,END} placeholder block for reload-time 'ip daddr <resolved-ip> accept' injection per Task 4.1.

packages/devbox/docker-compose.yml lines 91-99 iter-158: cap_add: [NET_ADMIN, NET_RAW] added to devbox service per SC-6 (defensive-explicit; container is currently root-with-all-caps so cap_add is a no-op pre-Story-2.5; locks the explicit allowance so Story 2.5's future cap_drop: [ALL] reduces to cap_drop:[ALL] + cap_add:[NET_ADMIN,NET_RAW] without breaking egress enforcement). Stale '# TODO(Story 2.3): add nftables / dnsmasq sidecar services' comment at line 10 replaced by factual in-container wiring descriptor (no sidecar service) per Task 9.1. NO cap_drop: [ALL], NO user: dev, NO security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true] — those belong to Story 2.5 (SC-6 scope-creep prohibition).

packages/devbox/Dockerfile lines 56-57: 'nftables' apt-appended (binary available after next image bake). iter-123 bake is pre-Story-2.3; re-bake deferred to operator workstation (Task 1.2 + 1.3; backend-B iteration-env re-bake denial per Story 2.1 iter-127 precedent).

Live-smoke AC 2 runtime verification (Task 12.3 + 12.4 SC-7 verbatim `nft list chain inet keel_egress output_v4 | grep -q 'policy drop'` + IPv6 equivalent) deferred to operator workstation per backend-B iteration-env constraint + kernel-nftables-privilege absence in DinD host-socket-passthrough layer. SC-7 verbatim commands pinned in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § Verification and packages/devbox/README.md § Egress policy § Verification for operator close-out.

requirements.title: Each DNS query is written to a structured JSONL file at a pinned path suitable for FR37 security-evidence persistence; log rotation is configured to prevent unbounded growth
substrate_evidence: packages/devbox/scripts/egress-log-tailer.sh authored iter-158 (7377 bytes; 0755; #!/usr/bin/env bash; set -euo pipefail; bash -n exit 0 at iter-159 verification): tails /var/log/dnsmasq.log via `tail -Fn0` (follow + new-file-on-rotation; start from end so historical lines are not re-emitted); parses each dnsmasq query-log line (query format '<timestamp> dnsmasq[<pid>]: query[<type>] <domain> from <client>'; reply format '<timestamp> dnsmasq[<pid>]: reply <domain> is <answer>'; block format '<timestamp> dnsmasq[<pid>]: config <domain> is <NXDOMAIN|0.0.0.0>') and synthesizes JSONL records per SC-3 6-field schema.

SC-2 pinned JSONL path /workspace/logs/egress-queries.jsonl written per SC-17 (start-egress.sh idempotent mkdir -p /workspace/logs; host-side bind-mount guaranteed). Epic 4 FR37 security-evidence emitter consumes this path; path pinned at 1.0 for downstream hard-reference.

SC-3 6-field JSONL schema (stable declared field order: timestamp/query/type/result/upstream/client; UTF-8; LF-terminated; one object per line) EMBEDDED VERBATIM in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § JSONL query log schema per iter-156 PATCH 3 — contentHash `aad16a51aa1dc7527c0312e6b99217966d7f3f3478fb677dd347792e9cb6889b` locks the 6-field contract against drift (Story 2.2 iter-151 AR-2 lesson: contract language scattered outside sourcePath drifts silently). Example line pinned: {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T12:34:56.789Z","query":"api.anthropic.com","type":"A","result":"allow","upstream":"1.1.1.1","client":"127.0.0.1"}.

SC-4 rotation mechanism: on every write, check filesize; if > 50 MB pinned threshold, rotate inline — close fd, rename egress-queries.jsonl → egress-queries.jsonl.1.tmp, gzip → egress-queries.jsonl.1.gz, shift older generations (.N.gz → .N+1.gz), drop anything beyond .5.gz, reopen fd to fresh empty egress-queries.jsonl. 50 MB + 5 generations ≈ 250 MB worst-case — well under KEEL_DEVBOX_TMPFS_LOGS_MB=500 default (Story 2.5 consumer). Avoids external logrotate daemon (container single-purpose discipline); gzip is stdlib.

SC-15 process model: egress-log-tailer.sh runs as background process launched from start-egress.sh via `nohup … >/dev/null 2>&1 &`; PID captured to /run/keel-egress-tailer.pid for clean `kill -TERM` on reload per Task 5.6.

SC-16 reload/rotation safety: rotation releases the open file handle BEFORE the nft/HUP reload completes (reload is orthogonal to rotation — rotation does not drop in-flight queries). Rotation cadence is event-driven per write; atomic reload script does NOT trigger rotation. SIGTERM/SIGINT handler flushes + closes cleanly (Task 7.5). Parse-error fallback: malformed dnsmasq log lines emit JSONL records with result=parse-error + raw line in 'raw' field — no silent drops (Task 7.6).

packages/devbox/scripts/monitor.sh authored iter-158 (881 bytes; 0755; bash -n exit 0 at iter-159): `exec tail -Fn0 /workspace/logs/egress-queries.jsonl | jq -c --unbuffered '.'` — operator-facing raw JSONL tail per SC-9 + AC 3 observability. No filter args, no format flags (Story 2.4/2.6 scope).

Live-smoke AC 3 runtime verification (Task 12.6 JSONL schema round-trip via `tail /workspace/logs/egress-queries.jsonl | jq -c .` + SC-3 6-field validation + Task 12.7 log-rotation smoke via synthesized 51 MB writes or local-uncommitted 1 MB threshold override) deferred to operator workstation per backend-B iteration-env constraint. SC-3 + SC-4 verbatim specifications pinned in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § JSONL query log schema for operator close-out.

requirements.title: An atomic reload is triggered via file-lock (mechanism used by Story 2.4's CLI); dnsmasq + nftables are re-loaded without dropping in-flight connections; the reload is atomic (either both layers apply the new policy, or neither does)
substrate_evidence: packages/devbox/scripts/reload-egress.sh authored iter-158 (8342 bytes; 0755; #!/usr/bin/env bash; set -euo pipefail; bash -n exit 0 at iter-159 verification): implements SC-5 atomic-reload contract verbatim — (1) argument contract `reload-egress.sh <composed-whitelist-path>` (exit 2 if arg missing; exit 3 if path unreadable per Task 6.2); (2) `flock -x 200` on /run/keel-egress.lock with 10s timeout (exit 4 if unavailable per Task 6.3); (3) render nftables ruleset — copy packages/devbox/nftables/egress.nft to temp file, replace KEEL_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST_MARKER_{START,END} with resolved IP allow-rules (for each domain: `getent ahostsv4` + `getent ahostsv6` → emit `ip daddr <addr> accept` + `ip6 daddr <addr6> accept` in appropriate chain per Task 6.4); (4) render dnsmasq conf — copy packages/devbox/dnsmasq/dnsmasq.conf to /etc/dnsmasq.conf, replace marker block with `server=/<domain>/${KEEL_DEVBOX_DNS_UPSTREAM:-1.1.1.1}` per Task 6.5; (5) apply ruleset via `nft -f <temp-rendered-file>` single kernel-atomic transaction (exit 5 if fails — previous ruleset stays active per Task 6.6); (6) reload dnsmasq via `kill -HUP $(cat /run/dnsmasq.pid)` with `pkill -HUP dnsmasq` fallback per Task 6.7; (7) release flock on exit via trap per Task 6.8; (8) exit 0 with one-line summary (`reload ok: N domains, M ipv4 rules, K ipv6 rules`) per Task 6.9.

SC-5 kernel-atomic guarantee: `nft -f <single-tempfile>` replaces the entire ruleset in one transaction; in-flight packets matching the old ruleset's `ct state established,related accept` rule continue to match the new ruleset's equivalent rule (baseline established,related rule preserved across reloads per egress.nft Task 4.1 design). `kill -HUP dnsmasq` re-reads /etc/dnsmasq.conf + whitelist files without daemon restart — preserves in-flight UDP/TCP DNS queries (UDP stateless; dnsmasq answer cache persists).

SC-5 failure modes: nft -f fails → previous ruleset stays active (kernel rollback); reload-egress.sh exits 5 before dnsmasq HUP; dnsmasq config unchanged. Atomicity preserved. nft -f succeeds but kill -HUP fails → new nftables active with old dnsmasq config (documented fallible seam per story v1.3 Completion Notes § Known residual risk). Mitigation: dnsmasq HUP rarely fails; pidfile-read failure falls back to pkill -HUP dnsmasq; if both fail, exit 7 with actionable stderr, operator may manually restart dnsmasq (Story 2.4 CLI will surface this via diff-summary). Accepted residual risk at 1.0.

SC-8 whitelist compose boundary: Story 2.3 ships the reload-egress.sh primitive taking a single composed whitelist filepath as argument + performs the atomic reload. Story 2.3 ships a static baseline composition (whitelist.default.txt + whitelist/*.txt concatenation, NO per-fork override) run once at container start via start-egress.sh. Story 2.4 later produces scripts/whitelist.sh user-facing CLI that handles per-fork override + validation + diff summary + invocation of reload-egress.sh. Story 2.3 MUST NOT create scripts/whitelist.sh (SC-8 scope prohibition).

SC-16 reload/rotation orthogonality: reload does NOT trigger rotation; rotation is event-driven per write in egress-log-tailer.sh; reload-egress.sh does not interact with the JSONL tailer process lifecycle.

Live-smoke AC 4 runtime verification (Task 12.5 atomic-reload preservation: long-running `curl --keepalive-time 60 https://api.anthropic.com/` in background + reload-egress.sh invocation on same whitelist → verify curl connection not broken AND reload exits 0 within 2s + timing + both exit codes) deferred to operator workstation per backend-B iteration-env constraint (requires live dnsmasq + running container + actual nftables kernel rule-load path). SC-5 verbatim specification pinned in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § Mechanism + § Verification for operator close-out.

requirements.title: Attempting to curl an unwhitelisted domain fails — DNS resolution fails (dnsmasq NXDOMAIN) AND TCP connection is rejected (nftables default-deny); upstream divergent-whitelist-script problem is closed (one mechanism, two enforcement layers)
substrate_evidence: Dual-layer belt-and-braces enforcement per architecture § S5 line 224 + PRD FR1a + NFR6 — verified at template level: (layer 1) dnsmasq default `address=/#/0.0.0.0` + `address=/#/::` in packages/devbox/dnsmasq/dnsmasq.conf (SC-12) returns 0.0.0.0/:: for any domain NOT in the allow-list `server=/<domain>/<upstream>` entries — fail-closed posture at DNS resolution layer; (layer 2) nftables `chain output_v4 { policy drop }` + `chain output_v6 { policy drop }` in packages/devbox/nftables/egress.nft (SC-7) drops any layer-3 packet not matched by a preceding allow rule — fail-closed posture at layer-3 enforcement.

SC-13 resolv.conf pin (start-egress.sh Task 5.3): /etc/resolv.conf overwritten to exactly two lines — 'nameserver 127.0.0.1' + 'options edns0 single-request-reopen'. NO upstream IP written to resolv.conf (no 8.8.8.8, no 1.1.1.1, no corporate resolver). Upstream ONLY reachable via dnsmasq's per-domain server= directives. Closes upstream cc-devbox bug #2 (fail-open resolv.conf fallback to 8.8.8.8).

SC-1 in-container runtime: dnsmasq + nftables run as in-container daemons/rules inside the devbox service, NOT as separate Compose sidecar services — matches AC 1 verbatim 'When the container starts, dnsmasq runs as the in-container DNS authority' + architecture § S5 single file-locked shell-script inside the container. Stale '# TODO(Story 2.3): add nftables / dnsmasq sidecar services' docker-compose.yml:10 comment replaced by factual in-container wiring descriptor per Task 9.1.

SC-8 single-mechanism collapse: Story 2.3 ships ONE reload primitive (reload-egress.sh); Story 2.4 is the sole operator entry point (whitelist.sh). Closes upstream cc-devbox bug #1 (divergent whitelist tooling — upstream shipped manage-whitelist.sh with /etc/whitelist-domains.conf + pkill -HUP AND a separate `whitelist` script with /workspace/.claude/whitelist + pkill + respawn — different state, different reload, intermittent unexpected blocks).

IPv4/IPv6 parity verbatim (SC-7 + AC 2 closure): address=/#/0.0.0.0 + address=/#/:: in dnsmasq + chain output_v4 + chain output_v6 both policy drop in nftables. Closes upstream cc-devbox bug #3 (IPv6 default-deny gap — upstream's whitelist.conf only blocked IPv4 address=/#/127.0.0.1; IPv6 AAAA queries bypassed).

Entrypoint fail-hard posture (entrypoint.sh line 101 per SC-11): hard-fails exit 1 if start-egress.sh is missing or non-executable — NO silent-allow path; fail-closed default per NFR6. Dev-agent Guardrail #1 (Fail-closed everywhere): start-egress.sh exits 1 if dnsmasq fails to start after 5s retry; reload-egress.sh exits 5/6/7 for nft-f/render/SIGHUP failures; entrypoint re-raises — verified at story file § Completion Notes § Guardrail compliance check.

packages/devbox/README.md § Egress policy § Known upstream bugs fixed subsection (Task 11.2): three-line list documenting divergent whitelist tooling + fail-open resolv.conf + IPv6 gap — hard-referenced in § Known upstream bugs fixed at lines 261-263 for 1.0 shipping documentation.

Live-smoke AC 5 runtime verification (Task 12.2 negative smoke: `curl -m 3 -sSf https://example.com/ 2>&1 | grep -Ei 'could not resolve|refused|timed out'` exits 0 + records stderr) deferred to operator workstation per backend-B iteration-env constraint. Plus Task 12.1 positive smoke (`curl -m 5 -sSf https://registry.npmjs.org/` exits 0 via npm allow-list) for round-trip verification. SC-12 + SC-7 combined verbatim commands pinned in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § Verification + packages/devbox/README.md § Egress policy § Verification for operator close-out.

recommendations.action: Accept WAIVED posture — five P2 ACs cover an infrastructure-security class story (third Epic 2 + first of its class) with no live test runner at 1.0. All 5 ACs have STRONG substrate evidence: (AC 1) dnsmasq.conf fail-closed `address=/#/0.0.0.0` + `address=/#/::` + listen 127.0.0.1,::1:53 + user=nobody privilege-drop + log-queries=extra + SC-12 allow-mechanism marker block + start-egress.sh SC-13 resolv.conf pin + .envrc.example KEEL_DEVBOX_DNS_UPSTREAM knob + Dockerfile apt-append + entrypoint fail-hard hook; (AC 2) egress.nft single inet keel_egress table with output_v4 + output_v6 chains both policy drop at priority 0 + SC-7 verbatim parity commands + cap_add NET_ADMIN/NET_RAW + nftables apt-append; (AC 3) egress-log-tailer.sh SC-3 6-field JSONL schema embedded verbatim in invariant doc + SC-2 /workspace/logs/egress-queries.jsonl pinned path + SC-4 50 MB + 5 gzip generations rotation + SC-15 background process model + SC-16 reload/rotation orthogonality + monitor.sh operator tail; (AC 4) reload-egress.sh SC-5 flock -x /run/keel-egress.lock + nft -f kernel-atomic + kill -HUP dnsmasq + pkill -HUP fallback + 8 documented exit codes + SC-8 whitelist-compose-boundary preserved; (AC 5) dual-layer fail-closed (dnsmasq NXDOMAIN + nftables layer-3 drop) + SC-13 resolv.conf pin (no 8.8.8.8 fallback) + SC-1 in-container runtime + SC-8 single-mechanism collapse + IPv4/IPv6 parity closure of upstream IPv6 gap + README § Known upstream bugs fixed documentation.
